es.example: Por otro lado, los costos suben.
es.exampleTranslation: On the other hand, costs are rising.

it.exampleTranslation: On the other hand, costs rise.

bridge.note: Both argue from sides, approached from different directions: Italian comes FROM the other side (da), Spanish goes BY it (por) — and only Italian keeps the article. Discourse markers are where prepositions go feral.

interference.es_to_it: DA pulls 'de otro lado' — regional at best; the pan-Hispanic marker is POR otro lado, and it drops the article.
interference.it_to_es: POR pulls 'per l'altro lato' — not the marker; Italian arrives FROM the other side: dall'altro lato (or d'altra parte).

review.notes: From Joe's Italian teacher's prepositions crash course, 2026-07-16. ES pairing chosen by Claude, unverified — reviewer to confirm.
